questions.question: What is the key CSS property for pure CSS parallax?
questions.explanation: perspective on the container enables the 3D space needed for parallax depth.

questions.question: In the perspective method, how do you make a layer scroll slower?
questions.explanation: Negative translateZ values place elements further away, making them scroll slower.

questions.question: Why use scale() with translateZ in parallax?
options: To compensate for size change from Z translation
questions.explanation: Elements moved away (translateZ negative) appear smaller; scale() compensates to maintain size.

questions.question: Which property is an alternative for parallax backgrounds?
questions.explanation: background-attachment: fixed keeps the background stationary while content scrolls.

questions.question: What issue does background-attachment: fixed have on mobile?
questions.explanation: background-attachment: fixed has limited or buggy support on mobile browsers.

questions.question: How many parallax layers are recommended for performance?
questions.explanation: 2-3 layers is recommended; more can cause performance issues.

questions.question: What overflow setting is needed for perspective parallax?
questions.explanation: The container needs overflow-y: auto or scroll to enable scrolling for the parallax effect.

questions.question: Does pure CSS parallax work on iOS Safari?
questions.explanation: iOS Safari has partial support and may need workarounds.

questions.question: What is an alternative to CSS-only parallax?
questions.explanation: JavaScript with scroll event listeners is the traditional approach for parallax.

questions.question: Which accessibility concern applies to parallax?
questions.explanation: Parallax scrolling can trigger vestibular disorders; respect prefers-reduced-motion.

questions.question: What is the scroll speed formula for a layer with translateZ(-2px) and perspective: 1px?
questions.explanation: Scroll speed = 1 + (translateZ / perspective) = 1 + (-2/1) = -1, absolute ratio ~0.33x.

questions.question: What is the scale compensation formula for parallax layers?
questions.explanation: scale = 1 + abs(translateZ) / perspective. For translateZ(-1px) with perspective: 1px, scale = 2.

questions.question: Which property must be set on the parallax container alongside perspective?
questions.explanation: transform-style: preserve-3d maintains the 3D context for child layers.

questions.question: What happens without scale() compensation in parallax?
options: Elements appear smaller and may leave gaps
questions.explanation: Elements with negative translateZ appear smaller; scale() compensates to maintain their visual size.

questions.question: Which is more performant: perspective method or background-attachment: fixed?
questions.explanation: The perspective method runs on the GPU, while background-attachment: fixed triggers repaints on every scroll.

questions.question: How do you disable parallax for prefers-reduced-motion?
options: Set perspective: none and transform: none on layers
questions.explanation: Disable perspective and reset transforms inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce).

questions.question: What is a common cause of the 3D parallax effect being flattened?
options: Overflow: hidden on a parent element
questions.explanation: Overflow: hidden outside the container flattens the 3D effect. The overflow must be on the container itself.

questions.question: What does a perspective value of 1px achieve in parallax?
options: Very close vanishing point for dramatic depth
questions.explanation: perspective: 1px places the vanishing point very close, creating noticeable parallax with small translateZ values.

questions.question: Which layer scrolls fastest in a CSS parallax setup?
options: All layers scroll at the same speed
The layer with the largest scale
questions.explanation: translateZ(0) elements scroll at normal speed (1x). More negative values scroll slower.

questions.question: What ARIA attribute should decorative parallax layers have?
questions.explanation: Purely decorative parallax layers should be hidden from screen readers with aria-hidden="true".
